Nothing will trouble the heart of a good person as much as the awareness of God’s anger. The way to keep your heart calm is to keep yourself in God's love. But a sense of guilt is too heavy to bear; and it would sink people into despair and ruin unless it is removed by God’s forgiving mercy. If there were no sin in our souls, there would be no pain in our bones, no sickness in our bodies. The guilt of sin is a burden to the whole creation, which groans under it. It will be a burden to sinners themselves when they are weighed down by it, or a burden of ruin when it casts them into hell. When we understand our true condition, the Good Physician will be valued, sought, and obeyed. Yet many allow their wounds to fester because they delay going to their merciful Friend. Whenever we are unwell in our bodies, we should remember how God has been dishonored in and by our bodies. The groanings which cannot be expressed are not hidden from Him who searches the heart and knows the mind of the Spirit. David, in his troubles, was a type of Christ in his agonies, of Christ on his cross, suffering and deserted.

Unrighteous people hate goodness, even when they benefit from it. David, in the complaints he makes about his enemies, seems to refer to Christ. But our enemies do us real harm only when they drive us from God and our duty. The true believer’s trouble will be made useful; he will learn to wait for his God, and will not seek relief from the world or himself. The less we notice the unkindness and injuries that are done to us, the more we promote the peace of our own minds. David's troubles were the chastisement and consequence of his transgressions, while Christ suffered for our sins and ours only. What right does a sinner have to yield to impatience or anger when mercifully corrected for his sins? David was very aware of the present workings of corruption in him. Good people, by constantly focusing on their sorrow, have been ready to fall; but by always focusing on God, they have maintained their standing. If we are truly sorry for sin, that will make us patient under affliction. Nothing is more distressing to a believer when in distress than to be afraid that God has abandoned him; nor does anything come more sincerely from his heart than this prayer, “Be not far from me.” The Lord will quickly help those who trust in Him as their salvation.
